title: An optical measuring and scanning system and methods of use
abstract: The present invention relates to an optical measuring system for fitting spectacles to a subject and/or for diagnosing and/or monitoring ocular diseases and/or disorder in the subject, and methods of use thereof. The system includes at least one image capturing device for capturing at least one image of at least part of the face of the subject. The system also includes at least one movable mount for mounting the image capturing device in front of the subject and moving the image capturing device relative to the subject; and at least one processor operatively connected to the image capturing device for generating a three dimensional (3D) model of the at least part of the face of the subject. The at least one processor is also configured to determine, from the 3D model generated, one or more optical measurements of the subject.
claims:
Claims 1. An optical measuring system for fitting spectacles to a subject, said system including: at least one image capturing device for capturing at least one image of at least part of the spectacles and at least one image of at least part of a face of the subject wearing the spectacles; at least one movable mount for mounting the image capturing device in front of the subject and moving the image capturing device relative to the subject; and at least one processor operatively connected to the at least one image capturing device, said processor configured to: generate 3D models of the at least part of the spectacles and the at least part of the face of the subject wearing the spectacles; align the 3D models generated; and determine, based on the 3D models once aligned, one or more optical measurements, including at least one of the visual axis, the MCOR and the OCR of each eye of the subject. 2. An optical measuring system for fitting spectacles to a subject, said system including: at least one machine recognisable tag associated with the spectacles; at least one image capturing device for capturing at least one image of at least part of the spectacles and at least one image of at least part of a face of the subject wearing the spectacles; at least one movable mount for mounting the image capturing device in front of the subject and moving the image capturing device relative to the subject; and at least one processor operatively connected to the image capturing device, said processor configured to: generate 3D models of the at least part of the spectacles and the at least part of the face of the subject wearing the spectacles; align the 3D models generated, based on the at least one machine recognisable tag; and determine, based on the 3D models once aligned, one or more optical measurements, including at least one of the visual axis, the MCOR and the OCR of each eye of the subject. 3. The system of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the at least one image capturing device is a stereoscopic system including at least two cameras spaced apart from one another. 4. The system of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the at least one image capturing device further includes at least one sensor and at least one emitter capable of emitting radiation selected from the group consisting of light, infrared, near infrared and X-rays or soundwaves in the form of ultrasound. 5. The system of claim 4 when dependent on claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the at least one emitter, in use, emits radiation or soundwaves that are reflected off the at least part of the face of the subject wearing the spectacles and/or the at least part of the spectacles for generating the 3D model of the at least part of the spectacles or the face. 6. The system of any one of claims 2 to 5, wherein the system includes at least three machine 

 recognisable tags, each tag being the form of an adhesive label configured to be adhered to a portion of the frame front of the spectacles in a triangular arrangement. 7. The system of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the at least one movable mount is configured to move the at least one image capturing device to image the at least part of the face of the subject over a range of at least 90Â°. 8. The system of any one of claims 1 to 7, further including a rail extending along a support surface for moving the at least one mount and the at least one image capturing device along the rail relative to the subject. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the rail extends at least in a lateral direction in front of the subject. 10. The system of claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the rail extends in a curve or arc at least partially about a front of the subject such that a centre of curvature of the curve or arc is defined at or near the subject. 1 1. The system of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the at least one processor is an external processing device selected from the group consisting of a computer, a tablet, a smart phone, a smart watch or a PDA. 12. The system of claim 1 1 , further including a wireless communication module for wirelessly connecting the at least one image capturing device to the external processing device. 13. A method of fitting spectacles to a subject, said method including: imaging at least part of the spectacles and at least part of a face of the subject wearing the spectacles to generate 3D models of the at least part of the spectacles imaged and the at least part of the face imaged; aligning the 3D models generated; and determining one or more optical measurements of the subject from the 3D models once aligned, including at least one of the visual axis, the MCOR and the OCR of each eye of the subject. 14. A method of fitting spectacles to a subject, said method including: associating at least one machine recognisable tag with the spectacles; imaging at least part of the spectacles and at least part of a face of the subject wearing the spectacles to generate 3D models of the at least part of the spectacles imaged and the at least part of the face imaged; aligning the 3D models generated based on the at least one machine recognisable tag; and determining one or more optical measurements of the subject from the 3 D models once aligned, 

 including at least one of the visual axis, the MCOR and the OCR of each eye of the subject. 15. The method of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the imaging at least part of a face of the subject wearing the spectacles includes imaging the subject while moving the image capturing device at least partially about, around or across the at least part of a face of the subject. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the imaging further includes imaging the at least part of the face of the subject wearing the spectacles while the subject is focusing on objects at different distances or lengths from the subject. 17. The method of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the imaging at least part of the spectacles is carried out with a handheld 3D scanner. 18. The method of any one of claims 13 to 17, wherein the MCOR of each eye is determined by modelling a sphere or ellipsoid over the sclera of the eye of the subject in the 3D model generated and determining the centre point of each sphere or ellipsoid. 19. The method of any one of claims 13 to 17, wherein the MCOR of each eye is determined by modelling more than one sphere or ellipsoid over the sclera of each eye of the subject in an averaged and integrated 3D model derived from 3D models of the sclera aligned relative to one another and averaged prior to being integrated with the 3D model of the subject focusing at a long focal length, wherein the centre point of each sphere or ellipsoid modelled is then determined and the position of the centre points averaged to determine an average centre point corresponding to the MCOR. 20. The method of any one of claims 13 to 19, wherein the visual axis is determined from the 3D model of the subject focusing at long focal lengths by axially extending an imaginary line along the line of sight through the centre of the pupil of the eye to a rear surface of the eye. 21. The method of any one of claims 13 to 20, wherein the OCR is determined for each eye by locating or determining the visual axis of each eye and then determining the shortest distance from the MCOR to the visual axis to thereby determine the OCR. 22. The method of any one of claims 13 to 21 , wherein the determining further includes measuring a monocular pupillary distance (PD) of each eye of the subject. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the PD of each eye is determined by measuring a horizontal distance in the 3D model generated from the visual axis of each eye to a vertical line of symmetry extending through a bridge of the spectacles. 24. The method of any one of claims 13 to 23, wherein the determining further includes measuring a 

 pupil height (PH) of each eye of the subject. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the PH of each eye is detennined by measuring a vertical distance in the 3D model generated from a centre of the pupil of each eye to a lowennost inside frame edge of the spectacles (i.e., for fully rimmed spectacles) or a lowermost outside frame edge of the spectacles (i.e., for rimless or partially rimmed spectacles). 26. The method of any one of claims 13 to 25, wherein the determining further includes measuring a back vertex distance (BVD) of each eye of the subject. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the BVD of each eye is determined by measuring a shortest distance in the 3D model generated between the apex of the cornea of each eye and a plane corresponding to the back vertex of the lenses of the spectacles. 28. The method of any one of claims 13 to 27, wherein the determining further includes measuring an optical centre of rotation distance (OCRD) of each eye of the subject. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the OCRD of each eye is determined by measuring a shortest distance in the 3D model generated from the OCR of each eye to a plane corresponding to the back vertex of the lenses of the spectacles. 30. The method of any one of claims 13 to 29, wherein the determining further includes measuring a pantoscopic tilt of the spectacles being worn by the subject. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the pantoscopic tilt is determined by measuring in the 3D model generated the angle between a plane corresponding to a frame front of the spectacles and a vertical plane extending perpendicular to the visual axis. 32. The method of any one of claims 13 to 3 1 , wherein the determining further includes measuring a frame wrap or face-form wrap of the spectacles being worn by the subject. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the frame wrap or face-form wrap of the spectacles is determined by measuring in the 3D model generated the angle between a plane corresponding to each of the left and right frame rims of the spectacles and a vertical plane extending perpendicular to the visual axis. 34. The method of any one of claims 13 to 33, wherein the determining further includes measuring a head cape of the subject. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the head cape is determined by measuring in the 3D model generated the horizontal angle of head rotation between the at least part of a face of the subject and the 

 subject's line of sight when the subject is standing or seated in a configuration such that the Frankfort plane (PF) associated with the subject's head is substantially horizontal. Date: 26 May 2017
